rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical chlorosulfinylation of styrenes with arenethiols giving β-chloro sulfoxides via radical/radical-polar crossover and in situ sulfide-to-sulfoxide oxidation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arenethiol/thiolate to thiyl radical (and further to disulfide/sulfide oxidation to sulfoxide); thiyl radical adds to styrene to give a β-thio C-radical that is oxidized to a benzylic cation and trapped by chloride
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSH to ArS• (and ArSSAr); ArS• adds to styrene to give a β-thio benzylic radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation and trapped by Cl- to deliver the β-chlorosulfide; subsequent anodic oxidation of the sulfide to the sulfoxide (with O incorporation from H2O) furnishes the β-chloro sulfoxide product.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 shows 18O incorporation (O16/O18 = 95:5 from a 90:10 H2 16O/H2 18O feed), indicating the sulfoxide oxygen originates from water

probe_reagents.name: (1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinyl)benzene (radical clock 56)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 57 obtained in 10% yield (dr 2:1), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: time-course sampling with 19F NMR (4-fluorothiophenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Detection and kinetic profiling of Int1-Int6 (disulfide, β-hydrosulfide, β-hydroxylsulfide, β-chlorosulfide, β-acetoxysulfide, β-hydroxylsulfoxide) as transient intermediates during electrolysis

probe_reagents.name: HCl additive control (sulfide → sulfoxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of a model sulfide gives sulfoxide in 11% without HCl vs 31% with HCl in 1,4-dioxane, indicating HCl promotes the S→S=O oxidation step

notes: 18O labeling confirms water (not O2 or AcOH) as the sulfoxide oxygen source. Time-resolved 19F NMR sampling reveals a cascade: ArSH → ArSSAr → β-thioether → β-functionalized (OH/Cl/OAc) sulfides → β-hydroxysulfoxide → final β-chloro sulfoxide. Radical clock supports a discrete C-radical, while Cl-/OH/OAc trapping points to a benzylic cation (radical-polar crossover). Cyclic voltammetry (Section 6.6) further supports anodic oxidation of the thiol/sulfide.
